---
description: 'Drafting-phase rules for the Requirements Builder agent: token substitution, per-framework output paths, and wrapper conventions.'
applyTo: '**/.copilot-tracking/requirements-sessions/**'
---

# Requirements Builder — Phase 4: Drafting

Phase 4 renders the templates referenced by selected FSI items into the per-framework output files declared by each framework's `index.yml.outputPath`.

## Token Substitution

* Token syntax: `{{name}}` per the FSI document-section contract (see [`framework-skill-interface`](../../skills/shared/framework-skill-interface/SKILL.md)).
* Resolve every token against (in order): the item's declared `inputs[].name`, the manifest `globals` map, and `state.intake.answeredQuestions[]` keyed by theme/id.
* Templates rendered through Docusaurus may use the escaped form `\{\{name\}\}` to bypass MDX parsing; the validator `Validate-FsiContent.ps1` normalizes the escape so tokens are still detected. Emit unescaped `{{name}}` into draft output files (the published template form is an authoring convenience, not a runtime form).
* Unresolved tokens block drafting; surface them as ❓ open questions and return to Phase 2 for the missing inputs.

## Per-Framework Output Paths

| Framework          | Output path                        |
|--------------------|------------------------------------|
| `requirements-prd` | `docs/prds/<slug>.md`              |
| `requirements-brd` | `docs/brds/<slug>-brd.md`          |
| Custom frameworks  | Declared in `index.yml.outputPath` |

`<slug>` is the kebab-case slug from `state.meta.slug`. New frameworks must declare their own `outputPath` in `index.yml`; the builder does not infer paths.

## Wrapper Conventions

Every drafted file:

* Begins with `<!-- markdownlint-disable-file -->` and the framework's standard frontmatter (when defined by the template).
* Includes a generation comment immediately after frontmatter:

  ```html
  <!-- generated by requirements-builder@<agentVersion> framework=<frameworkId>@<frameworkVersion> sha256=<intake-evidence-sha256> -->
  ```

* Wraps tabular regions in `<!-- markdown-table-prettify-ignore-start -->` / `<!-- markdown-table-prettify-ignore-end -->` when the template body does so.

## Hand-Edit Safety

Never overwrite a section that has been hand-edited since the last draft. Track per-section state via `state.drafts.outputs[].sectionsCompleted[]`:

1. Before re-rendering an item already in `sectionsCompleted[]`, compute the sha256 of the current section bytes in the output file.
2. Compare against the recorded `sectionsCompleted[].sha256`.
3. If they differ, ask the user to confirm overwrite (single ❓ question per drifted section). On ❌, leave the section as-is and remove it from `sectionsPending[]` with a `state.drafts.outputs[].sectionsSkipped[]` entry recording the drift hash and reason.

## Persistence

After each section is drafted:

* Append item id to `state.drafts.outputs[].sectionsCompleted[]` with `{ id, completedAt, sha256, sourceSkillId, sourceSkillVersion }`.
* Remove the id from `sectionsPending[]`.
* Append the read of any new template artifact to `state.skillsLoaded[]` and `skills-loaded.log`.

## Exit Criteria

Phase 4 is complete for a given framework when its `sectionsPending[]` is empty. The phase as a whole is complete when every selected framework reports empty `sectionsPending[]`. Append a one-paragraph summary listing per-framework output paths and section counts to `compaction-log.md` and advance to Phase 5.
